DISCOVERY 610, TENTATIVE RULING IS ADOPTED AS MODIFIED. PLAINTIFF STONESTOWN SHOPPING CENTER, L.P.'S MOTION FOR ORDER COMPELLING ANSWERS TO INTERROGATORIES AND SANCTIONS IS GRANTED. OPPOSITION IS UNTIMELY, AND RESPONSES WERE SERVED AFTER THE MOTION WAS FILED. SANCTIONS ARE REDUCED TO $1,000.00 PURSUANT TO CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 2030.290(C). MOVING PARTY WILL PREPARE AN ORDER. JUDGE: MONICA A. WILEY, NOT REPORTED.

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Sep-13-2010 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Compelling Answer
Off Calendar Jul-28-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Ivan M. Gold, Esq., for Plaintiff and moving party:
Fred S. Pardes, Esq., appearing by telephone, for Defendant and opposing party: Chicken Now Real Estate Group Stonestown Series

After hearing, tentative ruling is adopted as modified. PLAINTIFF STONESTOWN SHOPPING CENTER, L.P.'s Motion for Order Compelling Answers to Interrogatories and Sanctions is GRANTED. Opposition is untimely, and responses were served after the motion was filed. Sanctions are reduced to $1,000.00 pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure Section 2030.290(c). Moving party will prepare an order. Judge: MONICA A. WILEY, not reported.
